name: Romaine Salad with Orange & Radish
description: Inspired by a traditional Moroccan salad, which consists of oranges and radishes, this version adds romaine lettuce and a classic vinaigrette. Fresh orange juice adds acidity and brightness to the vinaigrette. Pomegranate seeds bring color and crunch. This recipe is perfect for a Kwanzaa celebration
ingredients: 2 small heads romaine lettuce, washed and torn into bite-size pieces

name: Garlic-Pecan Green Beans
description: The addition of toasted pecans gives this French way with string beans a bit of crunch and sweetness as well as a touch of the American South. Try using slender haricots verts for a different twist. This recipe is perfect for a Kwanzaa celebration
ingredients: 2 pounds fresh green beans, washed and stem ends removed

name: One-Pot Spaghetti with Meat Sauce
description: The whole family is sure to love this one-pot spaghetti recipe! The sausage, chicken broth and white wine impart slow-cooked flavors in this quick-cooking ragu. As the pasta sauce thickens, you'll want to scrape the bottom of the pot to prevent the sauce and pasta from sticking and to help the pasta cook evenly.
ingredients: 2 ounces sweet or hot Italian-style turkey sausage (2 links), casings removed
½ cup finely chopped yellow onion
1 (28 ounce) can no-salt-added crushed tomatoes with basil and herbs
½ cup water, plus more if needed
½ cup loosely packed fresh basil leaves, plus more for garnish

name: Herbed Chicken Thighs
description: Whether it's fried, boiled, roasted, baked or sautéed, chicken is eaten throughout Africa and the diaspora. In this recipe, chicken thighs are marinated in lemon juice and olive oil before being coated in herbs and broiled. The herbs will take on a slightly blackened color, so don't fret about the appearance. This recipe is perfect for a Kwanzaa celebration

name: One-Pot Beef Stroganoff
description: This one-pot beef stroganoff recipe cuts down on dishes, but not on flavor! Mixed mushrooms and sirloin steak layer on the umami flavors, while sour cream with a hint of Dijon mustard carries the sauce. Coating the steak in baking soda before cooking helps to tenderize it, but you can skip this step to speed things up if needed.
ingredients: 12 ounces sirloin steak, trimmed and sliced across the grain into 1/4-inch strips
12 ounces sliced fresh mixed wild mushrooms (such as cremini, shiitake and oyster)

name: Rhubarb Bread
description: This rhubarb bread has a lovely nutty flavor from the buckwheat flour which works beautifully with the tang from the rhubarb and Greek yogurt. The olive oil complements the buckwheat perfectly with earthy flavor. Crunchy bits of turbinado sugar on top add an extra hint of sweetness.
ingredients: ½ cup plain whole-milk strained yogurt, such as Greek or skyr
1 ½ cups fresh or thawed frozen rhubarb slices (1/4-inch)

name: One-Pot Chicken Pasta with Cajun Seasonings
description: This one-pot chicken pasta dish with Cajun seasonings is a snap to whip up. Here we use the technique of stirring often to help the starches release for natural creaminess. We love the spicy flavor profile of Cajun seasoning, but you can substitute different spices depending on what you have on hand.
ingredients: 4 medium scallions, thinly sliced, greens and whites separated
1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 3/4-inch pieces
12 ounces whole-grain penne or other short pasta
1 (15 ounce) can unsalted fire-roasted diced tomatoes, undrained
2 ounces reduced-fat cream cheese, cubed, softened

name: Vegan Chickpea Frittata
description: This vegan chickpea frittata is a creamy breakfast (or dinner!) dish loaded with veggies. The tomatoes give a burst of flavor in each bite, while the mushrooms add a savory note. The black salt is what gives this vegan frittata an egg-like flavor, but regular salt can be used in its place.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon kala namak (black salt) or regular salt (see Tip)
1 teaspoon reduced-sodium tamari or soy sauce (see Tip)
1 cup packed fresh spinach, roughly chopped

name: Ground Beef & Potatoes Skillet
description: In this healthy dinner recipe, ground beef and potatoes are paired with colorful veggies, including kale, tomato and peppers. Everything is cooked in one skillet, which allows for layers of flavor to build quickly while also cutting back on the number of dishes.
ingredients: 1 bunch lacinato kale, stemmed and roughly chopped

name: Caramelized Ripe Plantains with Dark Rum
description: Plantains are a common ingredient in West African cooking. From street snacks to stews, plantains can be enjoyed at every stage of their maturing process. Green plantains can be used for chips, while yellow ones serve as a starchy side. Here, we use super-ripe black ones where the starch has turned to sugar making them perfect for dessert. Serve these plantains warm for a dessert that all will love. This recipe is perfect for a Kwanzaa celebration
ingredients: ½ cup dark Barbadian or Jamaican rum

name: Layered Fall Salad with Squash & Mozzarella
description: The natural sweetness of the squash is balanced by tangy balsamic glaze in this gorgeous salad. We use delicata squash, but butternut or acorn would be a tasty substitution.
ingredients: 1 pound delicata squash, halved, seeded and cut into 1/2-inch slices
¼ teaspoon salt plus a pinch, divided
¼ teaspoon ground pepper plus a pinch, divided
3 ounces fresh mozzarella, thinly sliced and torn into pieces

name: Broccoli Casserole with Sun-Dried Tomato Cream Sauce
description: Sun-dried tomatoes pull double duty in this flavorful casserole: the oil is used to sauté the onion and garlic while the tomatoes are added to the sauce. The creamy sauce tops broccoli for a cheesy side dish that all will love.
ingredients: ½ cup slivered oil-packed sun-dried tomatoes, plus 3 tablespoons oil from the jar, divided
3 ounces cream cheese, at room temperature
1 ½ cups shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese, divided

name: Leftover-Turkey Hand Pies
description: These leftover-turkey hand pies are Thanksgiving all wrapped up in flaky pastry. Now you can enjoy all of your Thanksgiving favorites in one bite, using whatever leftovers you have in the fridge.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups chopped roasted turkey breast, leg, thigh or a mix
1 cup chopped cooked green beans, carrots and/or peas
1 (17.3 ounce) package frozen puff pastry sheets, thawed
All-purpose flour for dusting and rolling

name: Chicken, Spinach & Rice Casserole with Sun-Dried Tomato Cream Sauce
description: Cheesy, filling and utterly delicious, this weeknight-friendly casserole uses precooked chicken and brown rice to save time without sacrificing flavor. Be sure to squeeze as much water from the frozen spinach as you can so your casserole doesn't get watery.
ingredients: ½ cup slivered oil-packed sun-dried tomatoes, plus 3 tablespoons oil from the jar
1 (10 ounce) package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
3 ounces softened cream cheese, cut into pieces
1 cup shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese, divided

name: Whipped Hot Chocolate
description: Any kind of milk will work in this fun take on hot chocolate that's perfect for special occasions. For other flavor variations, try adding a drop of peppermint extract or a pinch of pumpkin spice or chili powder, depending on the season. You can even swap out half of the milk for coffee for a flavorful twist.

name: Creamy Boursin Pasta with Spinach, Mushrooms & Tomatoes
description: Boursin cheese adds delicious creaminess to this one-pan, veggie-packed pasta. We use the garlic-and- herbs flavor here, but other Boursin flavors like shallot and chive or cracked black pepper would be just as tasty.
ingredients: 1 (5.2 ounce) package Boursin with garlic and herbs

name: Creamy Tomato Soup with Tortellini
description: If you're a fan of the classic dipping duo of toasty grilled cheese and tomato soup then you'll want to try this combo: cheese-filled pasta floating in an earthy tomato base gives you that same craveable carb-and-creamy mashup, without all the crumbs. This family-friendly tomato tortellini soup is popular with kids, but adults will love it too--both its flavor and the fact that it requires less than 30 minutes of active time to prepare.
ingredients: 1 large leek, white & light green parts only, halved lengthwise, rinsed and sliced (see Tips)
4 cups low-sodium vegetable broth or no-chicken broth
1 (28 ounce) can Italian plum tomatoes, drained and chopped
1 (9 ounce) package cheese tortellini, fresh or frozen
½ cup chopped fresh basil or parsley

name: Slow-Cooker Mushroom Soup with Sherry
description: This comforting and creamy slow-cooker soup is loaded with earthy, umami flavor from the mushrooms and soy sauce. Puréeing only some of the slow-cooker mushroom soup gives the dish complex texture and eye appeal. Garnish with additional black pepper and chopped fresh thyme, if desired.
ingredients: 3 pounds assorted fresh mushrooms (such as cremini, portobello, shiitake, and button), sliced

name: Artichoke-Potato Gratin
description: This gratin dish has all of the craveable flavors of artichoke dip in a more sophisticated form. It makes a terrific side with any roasted meats or poultry.
ingredients: 1 medium leek, white and light green parts only, diagonally sliced 1/2 inch thick
2 cups finely grated Parmesan cheese, divided
2 ½ pounds russet potatoes, peeled and sliced 1/8 inch thick
2 tablespoons capers, rinsed and patted dry

name: Slow-Cooker Middle Eastern Meatballs with Raita
description: This meal features dense and tender lamb meatballs, creamy and refreshing cucumber raita--a traditional Indian sauce made with yogurt and vegetables--and whole-wheat couscous, a grain-like pasta that can be found at most supermarkets. Garnish these slow-cooker meatballs with fresh dill sprigs, and serve with lightly toasted pita, if desired.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon finely chopped garlic (from 3 garlic cloves)
1/2 cup, plus 2 tablespoons finely chopped yellow onion (from 1 onion)
½ cup shredded English cucumber (from 1 cucumber)

name: Bruschetta-Topped Crispy Baked Chicken
description: A fresh tomato-and-basil topping on panko-crusted chicken cutlets makes an easy meal for family or casual entertaining.
ingredients: ½ cup panko breadcrumbs, preferably whole-wheat
2 cups cherry tomatoes, cut into eighths

name: Roasted Salmon Caprese
description: This oven-ready recipe is snap to prep. Salmon fillets and cherry tomatoes roast side by side on one pan, then are drizzled with balsamic glaze to pull it all together.
ingredients: 1 pound salmon fillet, skin removed, cut into 4 pieces

name: Spanakopita-Stuffed Portobello Mushrooms
description: These stuffed portobello mushrooms have a filling inspired by the Greek spinach pie, spanakopita. To keep them quick, we ditched the phyllo, so they're great for an easy weeknight vegetarian dinner.
ingredients: 1 (10 ounce) package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
½ cup crumbled feta cheese, plus 1 tablespoon
4 medium portobello mushrooms, stems and gills removed (see Tip)

name: Pressure Cooker "Corned" Beef & Cabbage
description: Corned beef is brisket that has been pickled in spices for days. You can buy it ready-to-cook or corn your own at home, but both are serious sodium bombs, packing more than 500 mg per 3-ounce serving--nearly 25% of the recommended daily limit. For this healthier pressure cooker corned beef and cabbage, we start with a plain brisket and add seasonings ourselves to control the salt level. We also opt for a flat-cut end of brisket, instead of the thicker point cut, as it has less fat, making it easier to slice when cooked. An electric pressure cooker, such as an Instant Pot, is absolutely key to this recipe, allowing you to cook the brisket in one hour--as opposed to three hours or more in the oven. It also lets you sear and roast in one device, saving cleanup time.
ingredients: 2 ½ pounds flat-cut beef brisket, trimmed
1 small head green cabbage (2 pounds), cut into 8 wedges

name: Valentine's Day Party Board
description: Show your love this Valentine's Day with this beautiful Valentine's Day party board. It's full of sweet and savory treats from nuts to prosciutto-wrapped dates. And, of course, it includes the classic combo of chocolate and strawberries. Just grab your favorite platter and fill it with these easy, beautiful bites. Your loved ones will thank you!
ingredients: 6 ounces unsalted roasted pistachios in shells
4 thin slices prosciutto, cut into quarters
4 blood oranges, peel and pith removed, sliced crosswise

name: Vegan Pineapple & Coconut Baked Oatmeal
description: This dreamy vegan baked oatmeal is full of tropical flavors from coconut and pineapple, and it couldn't be easier to make. It's a satisfying family breakfast but also feels special enough for a Sunday brunch with company.
ingredients: 2 cups unsweetened nondairy milk, such as oat or coconut
¾ cup plain nondairy yogurt, such as oat, soy or cashew

name: Conversation Heart Cookies
description: Celebrate Valentine's Day with these heart-shaped sugar cookies! This recipe uses whole-wheat pastry flour, cream cheese and lemon extract to make a healthier (and delicious!) cookie perfect for decorating. Set out a plate to get the conversation started.
ingredients: 2 cups whole-wheat pastry flour, plus more for rolling
Red, yellow and blue natural liquid food coloring

name: Iced Lemon Cookie Energy Balls
description: Next time you have a Girl Scout Cookie craving, try these healthier no-bake cookies instead. Their bright and lemony flavor is balanced by sweetness from Medjool dates, and they're held together with millet and almond meal. Keeping the dough chilled and dusting your hands with confectioners' sugar will keep them from sticking to your hands while you shape them.
ingredients: ¼ cup confectioners' sugar, plus more for dusting

name: 20-Minute Chicken Cutlets with Creamy Pesto Sauce
description: Serve these creamy pesto chicken cutlets over your favorite pasta or zucchini noodles. This easy dinner recipe is sure to become a new family favorite.
ingredients: ½ cup finely chopped red onion

name: Caramel Delight Energy Balls
description: Think of these easy no-bake cookies as a healthy makeover of one of our favorite Girl Scout Cookies--chewy caramel, dark chocolate and toasted coconut come together with fiber-boosting oats instead of sugar and flour. And the best part? They take only 15 minutes from start to finish.
ingredients: ½ cup unsweetened, no-salt-added almond butter
6 tablespoons mini semisweet chocolate chips, divided

name: Philly Cheesesteak-Stuffed Portobello Mushrooms
description: We've taken the key ingredients of a Philly cheesesteak--onions, peppers, thinly sliced beef and provolone cheese--and piled them into portobello mushroom caps to make a meaty and satisfying low-carb main dish.
ingredients: 4 large portobello mushrooms, stems and gills removed (see Tip)
8 ounces beef strip loin, thinly sliced

name: Muffin-Tin Spanakopita Omelets
description: Spinach, onion and feta cheese flavor these Greek-inspired spanakopita omelets with crispy phyllo crusts.
ingredients: ⅓ cup finely chopped red onion
1 (10 ounce) package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry

name: Egyptian Lentils, Rice & Pasta (Koshari)
description: This kitchen-sink of a vegetarian dish was most likely invented in Egypt in the mid-1800s when Cairo was a major multicultural trade port, which could explain the similarities to Italian spaghetti as well as the Indian rice-and-lentil comfort food khichdi. Three toppings--a spicy tomato sauce (shatta), crispy onions and a garlicky vinegar (dakka)--are added to the koshari before serving, but in Cairo most people like them in separate bowls so they can season their bites one at a time. (Recipe adapted from Zooba Restaurant.)
ingredients: 1 ⅓ cups long-grain brown rice, such as brown basmati
1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added brown lentils, rinsed

name: Vegan Black-Eyed Peas
description: Two Cypriot specialties--good olive oil and fresh lemons--lend sunny flavor to this simple bean dish, served as a side in Cyprus. Chard adds a hearty touch. Top with a dollop of Greek yogurt, or nondairy yogurt to keep it vegan, for a meatless meal.
ingredients: 1 pound chard, trimmed and coarsely chopped
3 cups low-sodium vegetable broth or no-chicken broth

name: Hawawshi (Egyptian Hot Beef Sandwich)
description: This hawawshi--a hot sandwich that's a favorite Cairo street food--is filled with ground beef, vegetables, herbs and spices and adapted to be made in a home panini press. If you don't have one, cook it in a skillet over medium heat with another skillet on top, weighted down with a few 15-ounce cans. (Recipe adapted from Zooba Restaurant.)
ingredients: 1 Italian long hot pepper, seeded if desired

name: Wild Asparagus with Scrambled Eggs
description: Spring brings a lush carpet of blooms to the hills of Cyprus and sends food-loving foragers out hunting for wild asparagus. Cooked with eggs and potatoes, it's a simple scramble that's often served as one of several small plates at dinner. You can find wild asparagus in the U.S. as well;" otherwise, shop for stalks that are as young and tender as possible. (Adapted from Munevver Gurel.)
ingredients: 1 medium waxy potato, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
12 ounces asparagus, trimmed and cut into 3/4-inch pieces

name: Purslane, Cucumber & Tomato Salad with Mint
description: Purslane is a nutrient-packed green with a bright, lemony flavor and delicate crunch. Many gardeners in the U.S. consider it a weed, but Cypriots use it to make refreshing salads. If it isn't growing in your own yard, try calling a local vegetable farmer, who might be pleased to give some away, or you can order it from melissas.com or chefs-garden.com. Watercress makes a peppery substitute in this simple salad recipe.
ingredients: 3 cups trimmed purslane or 6 cups trimmed watercress
1 medium English cucumber or 2 Persian (mini) cucumbers, diced

name: Egyptian Tossed Salad
description: This crisp blend of vegetables is offered as a topping or side at Zooba, the Egyptian street food restaurant in New York City. We made it a stand-alone salad to stuff into pita with taameya (Egyptian falafel--see Associated Recipes) or serve with just about anything else. (Recipe adapted from Zooba Restaurant.)
ingredients: ⅔ cup diced green bell pepper

name: Egyptian Lentil Soup
description: Use red, yellow or even brown lentils to make this iconic and super-simple Middle Eastern soup. Skip green or black lentils, which won't soften enough to puree smoothly. (Recipe adapted from Zooba Restaurant.)
ingredients: 2 ½ cups yellow or red lentils

name: Slow-Cooked Lamb with Lemons & Pomegranate
description: A long braise in the oven yields fork-tender results for a tough cut of lamb. Cinnamon, oregano, garlic and lemon infuse the lamb shoulder with Mediterranean aromas, while a final scattering of pomegranate arils provides a burst of color and crunch.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon dried oregano or 1 teaspoon chopped fresh
1 (3 pound) bone-in lamb shoulder roast, trimmed
1 medium onion, peeled and quartered with root end intact

name: Venetian Spice Cookies (Bicciolani)
description: The intense blend of spices in these cookies is a tip of the hat to Venice's history as a conduit for the spice trade between Europe and the Far East.
ingredients: ½ cup (1 stick) cold unsalted butter, cubed

name: Fried Asparagus & Squash Blossoms
description: Fritto misto, mixed fried foods, is a typical Italian nibble. Be sure to salt the asparagus and blossoms as soon as they're out of the oil so that the crystals will stick to the food.
ingredients: 15 white and/or green asparagus spears, trimmed
15 squash blossoms, stems trimmed to 3/4 inch

name: Egyptian Falafel with Tahini Sauce (Taameya)
description: The Arabic word taameya translates to "tasty little bits." This version, made with fava beans, which are plentiful in the Nile delta, is thought by some to be the original falafel. (Recipe adapted from Zooba Restaurant.)
ingredients: 1 ¾ cups (10 ounces) skinless dried fava beans (see Tip), soaked overnight
1 teaspoon coriander seeds plus 6 tablespoons, divided

name: Traditional Greek Tahini Dip
description: Super creamy with the nutty flavor of sesame seeds, this homemade tahini sauce recipe is a popular way to begin a meal in Cyprus. Serve with crudité;"s and pita.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for garnish

name: Italian Rice & Peas (Risi e Bisi)
description: This risotto-like combo of rice and peas is made with a very cool technique that infuses the dish with one of the great tastes of spring.
ingredients: 1 ¾ pounds fresh English peas in pods
⅓ cup passito or other white dessert wine
1 ½ cups arborio or carnaroli rice

name: Shaved Artichoke Salad with Shrimp
description: Venice was built on saltwater marshes in the 6th century, and many local crops like the city's Sant'Erasmo artichokes, have a delicate taste of the sea. For this healthy salad artichokes are sliced thin--a great use for a mandolin if you have one--and served raw. When paired with the sweet-salty shrimp the combo evokes the flavors of the lagoon. For tender and tasty results, buy the smallest, freshest artichokes you can find.
ingredients: ½ pound large shrimp (26-30 per pound), peeled and deveined

name: Sweet & Sour Rabbit (Rabbit Saor)
description: Here rabbit (or chicken) is cooked in the style of sarde in saor, a classic dish where sardines are fried in olive oil, mixed with onions and raisins sautéed in the same flavorful oil and then finished with a jolt of vinegar. Juniper berries add a woodsy essence.
ingredients: 1 (3 pound) rabbit, skinned and cut into 6 pieces, or 3 pounds bone-in, skinless chicken thighs
½ cup passito or other white dessert wine plus 2 tablespoons, divided
¼ teaspoon ground white pepper, divided
6 cups greens, such as arugula, mâche, lamb's lettuce, spinach or watercress

name: Herb-Crusted Mediterranean Sea Bass
description: This whole fish smothered with herbs and buttery breadcrumbs is simple enough to let the flavor of the Mediterranean seafood shine through. If cleaning and prepping the fish for this sea bass recipe isn't your thing, most fish counters at larger markets will do it for you.
ingredients: 1 (3 pound) whole black bass or sea bass, cleaned, head and tail intact
3 tablespoons chopped fresh marjoram or oregano

name: Braised Greens with Lemon & Fennel (Yahnera)
description: These healthy braised greens are a flavorful side to add to your plate. If you prefer, instead of the croutons, you can add potatoes to the pan with the leeks, onions and fennel and cook for about 10 minutes before adding the greens.
ingredients: ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil plus 2 tablespoons, divided, plus more for garnish
2 large leeks, white and pale green parts only, thinly sliced and rinsed well
1 large fennel bulb, coarsely chopped, plus 2/3 cup chopped fennel fronds or fresh dill, divided
2 pounds mixed greens, such as spinach, sorrel and/or chard, stems trimmed and coarsely chopped

name: Baked Chickpeas with Orange, Lemon & Squash (Revithia me Kolokytha)
description: On Crete, chickpeas are commonly flavored with Seville orange, a pleasingly bitter variety grown locally. Orange zest along with lemon juice makes a good substitute. A little mustard deepens the flavor.
ingredients: ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for garnish
1 tablespoon Aleppo pepper or 3/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper
1 ½ cups low-sodium vegetable broth or no-chicken broth
1 cup coarsely chopped celery leaves, plus more for garnish

name: Barley Rusks (Dakos)
description: Malted barley grain is mainly used to brew beer and whiskey but is used here to give the barley rusks a sweet, nutty flavor. Look for it at home-brew stores or order it online, along with malted barley flour. Top these crunchy toasts with tomatoes, olive oil and feta, or serve them alongside dips like taramosalata and tzatziki. For breakfast, do as Cretans do and crumble them in a bowl and top with yogurt, fruit, nuts and seeds.
ingredients: 3 cups all-purpose flour, plus more as needed
1 cup coarsely ground malted barley grain or malted barley flour
2 (.25 ounce) envelopes (4 1/2 teaspoons) instant dry yeast
½ cup extra-virgin olive oil plus 2 teaspoons, divided
3 tablespoons grape molasses (see Tip) or 2 tablespoons honey plus 1 teaspoon balsamic vinegar

name: Lemony Samphire
description: Called sea beans in the U.S., samphire is crunchy seaweed that adds brininess to dishes and can be eaten raw or cooked. Look for it in gourmet markets or fish markets or order online at melissas.com.

name: Zucchini Patties with Herbs & Feta (Kolokythokeftedes)
description: Greeks nibble on these popular zucchini patties as a meze in tavernas while waiting for their main courses to arrive.
ingredients: 1 ¼ cups crumbled feta cheese (10 ounces)
⅔ cup finely chopped fresh dill
1 ½ cups dry breadcrumbs, preferably whole-wheat
Whole-milk plain Greek yogurt for serving

name: Bulgur Pilaf with Eggplant, Pepper & Tomatoes (Hondros me Melitzanes)
description: On Crete, this pilaf is often made with xynohondros, or cracked wheat simmered in soured goat's milk. A sprinkling of feta pays homage to that flavor.
ingredients: 1 medium eggplant (about 1 pound), cut into 1-inch pieces
3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil plus 1/2 cup, divided
1 large green bell pepper, cut in 1/4-inch pieces
2 teaspoons Aleppo pepper or 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper
½ cup chopped fresh mint, divided

name: Mashed Legumes with Onion, Capers & Radicchio (Sympetherio)
description: Radicchio, capers and onions brighten up this mashed lentil dish. Sympetheros means "father of one's son- or daughter-in-law," probably implying the "marrying" of different legumes in one dish. It's a meze served all over Greece. This recipe is inspired by the version Dimitris Mavrakis serves at his restaurant Kritamon in Archanes, near the spectacular Knossos archaeological site on Crete.
ingredients: ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil, divided
1 large red onion, chopped, plus 1 medium red onion, thinly sliced, divided
1 ½ cups skinless dried fava beans
⅓ cup capers plus 1 tablespoon brine
Aleppo pepper or crushed red pepper for garnish

name: Blue Cheese & Mushroom Pizza
description: This sheet-pan technique produces pizza that looks and tastes like it was made in a wood-fired oven. The trick is partially cooking the crust before topping it, then cranking up the broiler to get a golden brown and crispy crust.
ingredients: 1 pound whole-wheat pizza dough, at room temperature
12 ounces mixed mushrooms, such as oyster, maitake and/or shiitake, sliced (about 8 cups)
½ cup chopped spring onions or scallions

name: Korean BBQ Tempeh Grain Bowl
description: If you're a tempeh skeptic, you've probably never had the firm soy-based slabs slathered with a sweet-and-spicy barbecue sauce. We give this easy BBQ tempeh combo a punch of umami with tamari and add a little heat courtesy of gochujang. It's easy to put your own spin on this recipe;" see Variations (below) for some riffs.
ingredients: 8 ounces tempeh, cut crosswise into 16 pieces

name: Chopped Salad with Chicken & Avocado-Buttermilk Dressing
description: This herb-loaded green goddess-inspired dressing gets a color boost from creamy avocado. Make a double batch of this healthy salad dressing to keep on hand for salads throughout the week.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs, such as tarragon, mint and/or parsley
½ cup finely chopped red onion
⅓ cup dried cherries or cranberries

name: Spinach, Lima Bean & Crispy Pancetta Pasta
description: We opt for fresh spinach pasta here--it cooks quickly and adds a pop of color. Cooking lima beans in pancetta drippings infuses them with flavor.
ingredients: 1 (16 ounce) package frozen baby lima beans, thawed
¾ cup grated pecorino cheese, divided

name: Old Bay Salmon with Lemony Mashed Peas
description: Cooking butter until it browns adds a toasty, nutty flavor to this easy skillet salmon recipe. Here it gives Old Bay-rubbed salmon a chef-worthy finish--not bad for 20 minutes!
ingredients: ¼ cup crème fraîche or sour cream
1 ¼ pounds skin-on salmon, cut into 4 pieces

name: Forbidden Rice & White Kimchi Steak Salad
description: This steak salad doesn't feature a typical dressing, but tangy kimchi, savory pan-fried shallots and lemon juice pack it with flavor. Forbidden rice helps add more color and nutrition to this tangy dish.
ingredients: 8 ounces hanger or boneless rib-eye steak, trimmed
½ cup forbidden rice or brown rice
5 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, divided, plus more to taste
4 scallions, trimmed and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
½ cup white kimchi (baek kimchi) or fresh sauerkraut, drained
4 cups tatsoi, spinach or other tender greens

name: Port-Braised Endive with Rye Breadcrumbs
description: Sweet port softens the bitter edge of this endive appetizer that is sure to please.
ingredients: 4 medium heads Belgian endive (about 1 pound), trimmed and cut in half lengthwise

name: Crispy Roast Chicken & Carrots
description: Nothing says comfort like a roasted whole chicken, but it can take a while to cook. Using parts instead puts this easy roast chicken on the weeknight menu. Fresh herbs, lemon juice and capers in this Italian salsa verde sauce brighten things up.
ingredients: 4 pounds bone-in chicken parts or one 4- to 5-pound whole chicken
1 cup chopped fresh herbs, such as basil, parsley, oregano and/or marjoram

name: Cajun Skillet Pork Chops with Asparagus & Cheesy Polenta
description: The Cajun pork chop rub adds a delicious smoky flavor to this easy skillet dish. This polenta has a nice sharp bite thanks to Cheddar cheese. Don't have any on hand? Try Parmesan or Gouda instead.
ingredients: 1 ½ pounds thin-cut bone-in pork chops
½ teaspoon salt plus a pinch, divided
2 tablespoons grapeseed or canola oil, divided
1 ¼ cups shredded sharp Cheddar cheese

name: Plant-Based Smashed Burgers
description: Learning how to make smashed burgers is as simple as its name. Smashing burgers into thin patties not only makes them quick-cooking, it also creates irresistible crispy edges. Topping them with the caramelized onion relish adds extra deliciousness.

name: Skillet Beef Potpie with Buttermilk Biscuits
description: Using an oven-safe skillet (like cast iron) allows you to take this healthy homemade potpie from stovetop to oven without dirtying extra pans. A quick drop-biscuit topping made with white whole-wheat flour adds 2g fiber per serving and cuts the sodium in half compared to using refrigerated biscuit dough.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons white whole-wheat flour plus 1 cup, divided
½ cup chopped fresh parsley, plus more for garnish
4 tablespoons cold unsalted butter (1/2 stick), cut into 1/4-inch pieces

name: Chile-Lime Cauliflower Quesadillas
description: Poblano peppers add a touch of heat to these vegetarian quesadillas, but a sweet bell pepper is tasty, too, if you want something milder. Start these quesadillas in the oven and finish them off in a skillet for melty cheese and crispy tortillas.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon tablespoon chili-lime seasoning or rub, such as Tajín (see Tip)
8 (6 inch) corn or whole-wheat tortillas, warmed
½ cup reduced-sodium refried black beans

name: Savory Dutch Baby with Arugula & Parmesan
description: Also called a German pancake, this breakfast or brunch dish is delicious served with sweet or savory ingredients. Here, we make this Dutch baby recipe dinner-worthy by topping it with a fresh salad, cheese and fried eggs.

name: Ginger-Orange Chicken Thighs with Baby Bok Choy
description: Searing the chicken before roasting at high heat ensures super-crispy skin. Combine that with mandarin oranges and you've got a healthy orange-chicken dish reminiscent of the favorite fried one at the mall food court--but without the fryer.
ingredients: 1 ½ pounds baby bok choy, halved
1 (11 ounce) can mandarin oranges in light syrup, drained
2 tablespoons Shaoxing rice wine or dry sherry, divided

name: Dilly Pickled Snap Peas
description: A pickling brine gives these fresh peas a bracing pucker. Serve in place of pickles on a sandwich or as an addition to a cheese board.

name: Chicken Mole Tacos with Mango-Jicama Slaw
description: Jicama stands in for cabbage in the slaw topping for these homemade chicken mole tacos. Its crunch complements the juicy mango without getting soggy.
ingredients: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts, trimmed
2 tablespoons lime juice, plus lime wedges for serving
¼ teaspoon thinly sliced red onion

name: Watermelon Juice
description: Clean and simple, it just takes a blender to whip up this DIY watermelon juice. You can enjoy this refreshing drink with no added sugar on its own, or jazz it up with one of our variations (see below).

name: Sweet Potato-Black Bean Burgers
description: These vegan sweet potato-black bean burgers spiced with curry powder are easy to make. Blending the mixture with your hands gives you a soft, uniform texture then the outside gets crispy by cooking in a cast-iron pan. To make this recipe gluten-free too, use gluten-free oats and serve the patty in a lettuce wrap, omitting the bun.
ingredients: 1/2 cup plain unsweetened almond milk yogurt

name: Vegetarian Enchilada Casserole
description: Think of this vegetarian enchilada casserole as a veggie-packed Mexican lasagna with corn tortillas standing in for the noodles! If your peppers are mild and you like heat, opt for spicy pico de gallo. This easy vegetarian dinner recipe is sure to become a new family favorite.
ingredients: 1 medium yellow squash, halved and sliced (1/4-inch)
1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added pinto beans, rinsed
1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added black beans, rinsed
1 ½ cups shredded pepper Jack cheese

name: Classic Slow-Cooker Stuffing
description: Save space in your oven and use your slow cooker to whip up this easy, classic stuffing. Hearty whole-grain bread gets toasted in the oven for only 10 minutes (and you can toast the cubes ahead of time!) while your slow cooker does the rest.

name: Vegan Black Bean Burgers
description: These easy vegan black bean burgers have a healthy dose of fiber thanks to the combination of black beans and quinoa. Cumin and chipotle chile powder give these vegan burgers a Southwestern spin. Using your hands to combine the mixture creates a soft, uniform texture.
ingredients: 1 (15.5 ounce) can no-salt-added black beans, rinsed

name: Dairy-Free Pumpkin Pie
description: One of the tasters of this dairy-free pumpkin pie called it a "vegan's dream come true." The filling has just the right amount of spice, while the crust--made with part whole-wheat flour--has a wonderful nutty flavor and crispy texture. The pie is great as is, but to really please the plant-based eaters at your Thanksgiving table, top it with Coconut Whipped Cream (see Associated Recipes).
ingredients: ½ cup vegetable shortening, cubed and frozen
¾ cup light coconut milk (see Tip)
¾ cup packed light brown sugar
1 (9 ounce) tub Dairy-free coconut whipped topping, thawed

name: Mushroom-Swiss Turkey Burgers
description: In this gluten-free turkey burger recipe, lean ground turkey stands in for ground beef, and portobello mushrooms produce a juicy, flavorful alternative to the traditional bun. Melted Swiss cheese, sliced tomato and arugula top off this delicious low-carb dinner!
ingredients: 8 portobello mushroom caps, stems and gills removed (see Tips)

name: Strawberry-Rhubarb Jam
description: Tart rhubarb and sweet strawberries shine together in this easy strawberry-rhubarb jam. There's no canning involved and with fresh in-season strawberries, added sugar stays in check.
ingredients: 2 pounds fresh strawberries, hulled and halved (6 cups)
3 cups chopped rhubarb, fresh or frozen (thawed)

name: Spicy Shrimp Tacos
description: These spicy shrimp tacos owe their kick to a smoky chipotle shrimp seasoning blend. Cooling avocado and tangy pico de gallo round out the flavor. This vibrant, easy dinner comes together in a flash, making it perfect for busy weeknights.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups thinly sliced red cabbage

name: Shrimp Seasoning
description: Making homemade shrimp seasoning blend is easy and lets you control exactly what's inside. Use it in place of the packaged seasoning blends to bring zesty flavor to shrimp as well as chicken, beef and more.

name: S'more Parfaits
description: This chilled dessert offers all the flavors of a s'more, but you don't need a campfire. It also features a protein boost from the Greek yogurt.
ingredients: ¾ cup crushed graham crackers (about 3 crackers)

name: Chicken Enchiladas with White Sauce
description: In this easy chicken enchiladas recipe, we make a white sauce from chicken broth and sour cream that coats corn tortillas rolled up with shredded chicken, onion, peppers and spices. Poblano peppers tend to be mild, but if you really want to cut the heat, use a green bell pepper instead.
ingredients: ¾ cup shredded pepper Jack cheese, divided

name: Grilled Green Panzanella Salad with Halloumi, White Beans & Vegetables
description: Panzanella is a Tuscan chopped salad of bread and tomatoes. This upscale version features halloumi cheese and is super easy because most of the ingredients are grilled, including the cheese.
ingredients: 2 medium (8 oz. each) zucchini, sliced lengthwise into 1/4-in. planks
6 ounces whole-wheat or whole-grain bread (from 1 [8-oz.] loaf), cut into 1/2-in.-thick slices
4 ounces halloumi cheese, cut into 1/4-in.-thick slices
¾ cup drained and rinsed no-salt-added white beans (from 1 [15-oz.] can)
1 cup loosely packed fresh basil leaves

name: Grilled Greek Chicken & Vegetable Salad
description: Fresh herbs, olives, and feta cheese give this grilled chicken and vegetable salad its distinct Greek flavor. On a hot summer evening when you don't want to be in the kitchen, fire up the grill and in 25 minutes, dinner is served.
ingredients: 1 medium red onion, cut crosswise into 1/2-in. rounds
1 large yellow squash, cut lengthwise into 1/2-in. planks
1 cup drained and rinsed no-salt-added chickpeas (garbanzo beans)
1 ½ ounces feta cheese, crumbled (about 1/3 cup)

name: Creamy Mushroom & Tomato Farro Risotto
description: Instead of arborio rice, this creamy grain dish features the bold nuttiness of farro, which pairs nicely with the black pepper and Parmesan. The cherry tomatoes add a nice touch of juicy sweetness, and the cremini mushrooms give a "meaty" feel to this vegetarian main dish.
ingredients: 3 cups lower-sodium vegetable broth (such as Imagine)
6 ounces sliced fresh cremini mushrooms (about 2 cups)
1 ¾ cups uncooked quick-cooking farro (about 8 3/4 oz.)
½ cup (2 oz.) chopped yellow onion (from 1 [5-oz.] onion)
2 ounces Parmesan cheese, grated (about 1/2 cup), divided

name: Whole-Grain Orzo & Grilled Vegetable Salad
description: This lovely Mediterranean orzo salad is rich with basil, grilled vegetables and tender fresh mozzarella cheese. The combination of vegetables, vinaigrette and fresh basil is a perfect base for the orzo and the cheese.
ingredients: 1 medium eggplant, unpeeled, sliced lengthwise into 1/2-in. planks
1 medium (8 oz.) zucchini, sliced lengthwise into 1/2-in. planks
1 medium (4 oz.) red bell pepper, quartered lengthwise
¼ cup refrigerated reduced-fat basil pesto (such as Buitoni)
1 (8 ounce) package fresh small mozzarella cheese balls
½ cup thinly sliced fresh basil

name: Peach & Blueberry Galette
description: With summery, bright fruit, this pie is a crowd- pleaser and pretty enough to be the centerpiece for a summer dinner party. The crust is tender, with a deeper flavor from the whole-wheat flour than you would get with a plain white-flour crust.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups (about 5 1/4 oz.) whole-wheat pastry flour
4 cups fresh peeled peach slices (about 1 lb.) or 1 (20-oz.) pkg. frozen peach slices, thawed
1 ½ tablespoons all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
1 tablespoon egg white (from 1 egg), lightly beaten

name: Sheet-Pan Ratatouille
description: Ratatouille is a type of veggie-forward, Provenç;"al-style stew. Our sheet-pan ratatouille features tangy goat cheese and sweet balsamic glaze that complement the tender charred vegetables. Bake the tomatoes on a separate pan so that the steam from the tomatoes won't steam the other vegetables.
ingredients: 1 large (1 lb.) eggplant, unpeeled and cut into 1-in. chunks (5 cups)
2 medium zucchini, unpeeled and chopped (2 3/4 cups)
2 medium red bell peppers, chopped (2 cups)
5 medium plum tomatoes, roughly chopped (5 cups)

name: Crispy Fish Tacos with Charred Corn & Crema
description: These tasty fish tacos offer all you need in a taco--they're crunchy, spicy, creamy and acidic, all in perfect balance. The corn and crema add a little extra flair to this fish taco recipe, making it perfect for guests.
ingredients: 2 ears fresh corn, each halved crosswise
¾ pound skinless snapper fillets, cut into 3/4- x 2-in. strips
2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh cilantro, plus cilantro leaves for garnish

name: BBQ Chicken Bowls
description: These BBQ chicken bowls are perfect for weeknight dinners. They come together in just 15 minutes and are chock-full of the classic barbecue flavors you love, including saucy beans, coleslaw and potatoes.
ingredients: 8 ounces Yukon Gold potatoes, cut into 1/2-in. pieces
2 (15 ounce) cans no-salt-added pinto beans, drained
1 ½ cups shredded cooked chicken breast
½ cup shredded, cooked chicken thigh
½ cup fresh yellow corn kernels

name: Chicken Caesar Lettuce Cups
description: Upgrade your work lunch with these tasty Caesar salad lettuce cups. This Caesar salad recipe gets a healthy makeover, thanks to homemade dressing made with Greek yogurt.
ingredients: 4 ounces sourdough bread, cut into 1/2-in. pieces (about 2 1/2 cups)
¼ cup 2% reduced-fat plain Greek yogurt
¼ cup finely shredded Parmesan cheese
2 cups chilled, chopped roasted chicken breast (about 6 oz.)

name: Creamy Spinach Pasta
description: This might be one of the creamiest light pasta dishes you've ever had! The mascarpone cheese adds a richness to the spinach pasta that's unexpected--especially in a healthy pasta recipe.
ingredients: 12 ounces uncooked tube-shaped chickpea pasta (about 3 1/2 cups) (such as Banza)

name: Turkey Meatballs with Green Beans & Cherry Tomatoes
description: The flavor of the turkey meatballs is enhanced by the Asiago and prosciutto. We love keeping this turkey meatball recipe on hand for meal prep, since meatballs can be used in sandwiches, salads, pasta and more.
ingredients: 1 ½ ounces Asiago cheese, finely shredded (about 2/3 cup)
1 ¼ ounces sliced prosciutto, finely chopped
¼ cup finely chopped yellow onions
¼ cup chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley, plus more for garnish
12 ounces haricots verts (French green beans), trimmed

name: Sheet-Pan Shrimp Fried Rice
description: Classic shrimp fried rice gets amped-up flavor with sambal heat. The textures are pretty spot on with hits of crispy and tender rice and are the same as you'd get if you prepared it in a wok or skillet. This sheet-pan shrimp fried rice recipe is a great way to use leftover rice, but you could also use pre-cooked microwave rice so that it can be coming to room temperature while you chop the veggies.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon sambal oelek (ground fresh chile paste)
3 cups cooked long-grain white rice, at room temperature
1 cup finely chopped red bell pepper
½ cup finely chopped yellow onion
½ cup plus 2 Tbsp. sliced scallions
1 pound large peeled, deveined raw shrimp

name: Crispy Roast Chicken
description: Classic and delicious, this roast chicken recipe will shine on the table fresh from the oven, served with any side. And this oven-roasted chicken is basic enough to play well in any recipe that calls for cooked chicken.
ingredients: 1 (4 1/2- to 5-lb.) whole chicken, giblets removed and discarded
½ small lemon, cut into wedges
½ small yellow onion, cut into wedges

name: Instant Pot Orange-Jalapeño Chicken Wings
description: These sweet-hot tender Instant Pot chicken wings boast a balance of orange and lime. They're very tangy and very sticky, so be sure to serve with lots of paper towels!
ingredients: 2 pounds chicken drumettes (about 24 wings), skin removed
1 medium jalapeño chile, seeded and finely chopped (2 Tbsp.)

name: Instant Pot Beef & Poblano Tostadas
description: Serve the tangy, peppery beef on crispy tostadas and top with colorful slaw.
ingredients: 1 (2 1/2 pound) boneless beef chuck roast, trimmed
½ cup plus 2 Tbsp. white vinegar, divided

name: Make-Ahead Chicken Pasta Salad
description: Need something to bring to a party or backyard barbecue? This make-ahead chicken pasta salad recipe is classic and completely adaptable-you can swap in whatever veggies, herbs or cheese you like.
ingredients: 8 ounces haricots verts (French green beans), cut into 2-in. pieces
2 cups shredded, cooked chicken breast (about 6 oz.)

name: 25-Minute Chicken & Veggie Enchiladas
description: These chicken enchiladas are great for using up any veggies you have lingering in your fridge. Our chicken enchilada recipe calls for zucchini, squash and onion, but you could easily swap in spinach, onions or potatoes.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups shredded, cooked chicken breast (about 4 1/2 oz.)
½ cup shredded, cooked chicken thigh (about 1 1/2 oz.)
4 ounces Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (about 1 cup), divided

name: Instant Pot Lowcountry Seafood Feast
description: Use your multicooker for a traditional seafood boil dinner that doesn't feel light by any means. The potatoes are perfectly tender, the sausage is cooked and still juicy and flavorful, the corn is tender but not overcooked, and the shrimp are tender and pink.
ingredients: 1 (12 ounce) bottle light beer or lager beer
8 ounces red baby potatoes, unpeeled and halved
8 ounces smoked turkey sausage, sliced into 1-in. pieces
2 ears fresh corn, shucked and cut crosswise into quarters
2 tablespoons salt-free Creole seasoning (such as Tony Chachere's), divided
1 pound medium unpeeled raw shrimp, tail-on

name: Instant Pot Summer Corn & Crab Chowder
description: Sweet corn and tender lump crabmeat go together wonderfully to make this rich, hearty chowder. This colorful, quick and easy Instant Pot soup makes a great starter for a summertime meal.
ingredients: 4 cups thawed frozen corn or fresh corn kernels (4 ears)
1 small russet potato, unpeeled and cut into 1/2-in. cubes
½ teaspoon cayenne pepper, or to taste
1 pound fresh lump crabmeat, drained and picked over

name: Steak, Pepper & Sugar Snap Stir Fry
description: Turn to this beef stir-fry recipe for a quick and easy dinner. The tender, spicy steak is cooked in the same wok or skillet as the crisp, colorful vegetables and smells divine as the dish is cooking.
ingredients: 1 cup sugar snap peas, sliced in half lengthwise
1 cup sliced yellow bell pepper (about 1 medium bell pepper)
1 Chopped fresh cilantro and crushed red pepper

name: Instant Pot Garlic-Scented Chicken & Farro with White Balsamic Vinaigrette
description: Pair earthy, toothsome barley with tender chicken dressed in a tangy white balsamic vinaigrette for a hearty main dish. This Instant Pot chicken recipe comes together in just 40 minutes, making it perfect for easy weeknight dinners.
ingredients: 4 small bone-in, skinless chicken breasts (about 3 1/4 lbs.)
1 ½ cups uncooked quick-cooking barley (from 1 [8-oz.] pkg.)

name: One-Pan Spicy Okra & Shrimp
description: Serve spicy blackened shrimp and tender vegetables over aromatic basmati rice for an easy skillet supper. Use precooked microwave rice so you don't have to mess up another pan. This one-pan dinner is perfect for company, but easy enough to make on a weeknight.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons salt-free Cajun seasoning (such as Tony Chachere's)
1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon olive oil, divided
½ pound fresh okra, halved lengthwise
1 ¼ pounds large shrimp, peeled and deveined

name: One-Pan Pork Tenderloin with Heirloom Tomato & Shallot Confit
description: Pair juicy, tender pork with a rich, flavorful tomato confit that tastes like melted tomatoes and sweet shallot preserves. We think this sweet and savory pork recipe is just as suitable for company as it is for Wednesday night dinner at home.
ingredients: 1 ½ teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary, divided

name: Instant Pot Adobo Chicken Thighs with Bok Choy & Green Onions
description: Use your multicooker to make a quick version of this Filipino dish that's traditionally marinated overnight. The sweet and spicy Instant Pot chicken is tender and juicy, while the bok choy keeps its flavor and crisp texture.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon salt-free garlic-and-herb seasoning blend (such as Mrs. Dash)

name: Easy Shrimp Scampi with Zucchini Noodles
description: Enjoy classic shrimp scampi lightened up with a white wine-butter sauce and zucchini noodles in place of pasta. The tomatoes add some sweetness and color, while the cheese contributes nuttiness and richness.
ingredients: 1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined

name: Instant Pot Garlic & Rosemary Roast Pork Loin
description: It's remarkable that a pork roast can be so good so quickly! The gravy is rich and flavorful and the meat is moist and perfectly cooked--using an Instant Pot is a real game changer for pork loin roast.
ingredients: 2 pounds boneless pork center-cut loin roast
1 tablespoon , plus 3 tsp. chopped fresh rosemary, divided

name: Whole-Grain Spaghetti with Italian Turkey Sausage, Arugula & Balsamic Tomato Sauce
description: Total happiness in a bowl: pasta, sausage, tomatoes and herbs. The tomatoes get sweet and tender and turn into sauce with the stock and the pasta. Without ever adding any butter, the spaghetti sauce is rich and silky. Adding the herbs at the end gives the dish a fresh pop of color and flavor. This is one healthy pasta recipe we can definitely get behind!
ingredients: 6 ounces sweet or hot Italian turkey sausage, casings removed
12 ounces cherry tomatoes or multicolor cherry tomatoes, halved if very large
8 ounces whole-grain spaghetti noodles, broken in half
¼ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese

name: Vegan Macaroni Salad
description: You don't have to be vegan to enjoy this vegan macaroni salad! The colorful array of veggies give it texture and flavor and help make it lighter than traditional pasta salads. It's perfect to bring along to your next picnic or potluck.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups whole-wheat elbow macaroni (about 6 ounces)
½ cup diced red bell pepper

name: Quinoa-Black Bean Salad
description: Enjoy this quinoa and black bean salad as a delicious and quick vegetarian main dish or as a side for grilled chicken or steak. And don't forget the leftovers! They make an easy lunch on the go.
ingredients: 1 medium zucchini, cut lengthwise into 1/4-inch planks
1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added black beans, rinsed
½ cup chopped fresh cilantro, divided
¾ cup crumbled cotija cheese, divided

name: Loaded Baked Potato Salad
description: This loaded baked potato salad recipe marries traditional potato salad with all the fixing of a classic stuffed potato. It's creamy and decadent thanks to sour cream, mayo, bacon and Cheddar cheese. Take it along to your next picnic or potluck--friends and family will love it!
ingredients: 2 ½ pounds baby red potatoes, cut into 1-inch cubes
3 slices thick-cut bacon, cooked and crumbled
½ cup shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
3 tablespoons finely chopped fresh chives, divided

name: Jalapeño Margaritas
description: This reduced-sugar jalapeñ;o margarita is a drink with a kick! We sweeten this zippy cocktail with fresh orange juice and just a touch of simple syrup steeped with slices of jalapeñ"o. If you like the heat, add more chile slices to the sugar mixture.
ingredients: 4 to 6 slices jalapeño pepper, plus more for garnish

name: BBQ Pork & Smoked Gouda Quesadillas
description: These satisfying quesadillas get a smoky flavor from the cheese and pork, sweetness from the sauce, and a crisp crunch from the red onion. The pickled jalapeñ;"os offer a twist on a typical barbecue sandwich pickle and add a touch of heat.
ingredients: 4 ounces smoked Gouda cheese, shredded (about 1 cup)
¼ cup thinly sliced red onion

name: Baked Potatoes on the Grill
description: Ever wonder how to make baked potatoes on the grill? It's easy! Just wrap the potatoes in a double layer of foil (which keeps in the steam that cooks your potato) and transform your gas or charcoal grill into an oven. The result: a lightly smoke-flavored skin and a fluffy tender potato on the inside that you can flavor with a dollop of sour cream and your favorite seasonings.

name: Loaded Sheet-Pan Nachos
description: This quick and easy Super Bowl recipe has been referred to as "the best nachos people have ever had." With easy-to-find ingredients, an optional pop of tang and spice from the pickled jalapeñ;"os, and the salsa verde and sour cream for serving, we think it's earned its title!
ingredients: 1 (8 ounce) package shredded Mexican blend or Cheddar cheese
¼ cup roughly chopped fresh cilantro
Salsa verde and sour cream for serving

name: Mediterranean Quinoa Salad
description: This Mediterranean quinoa salad is chock-full of fresh veggies that marinate in a bright, fresh dressing. It's an easy vegetarian dinner recipe that can also be made ahead as a healthy lunch option.
ingredients: 3 cups cooked quinoa, cooled (see Associated Recipes)
1 ½ cups thinly sliced red onion
½ cup halved pitted Kalamata olives

name: Cheesy Mushroom Gratin
description: Earthy portobellos are roasted then smothered in cheese in this easy low-carb side dish. Enjoy this gratin alongside pork or steak, or add a salad and turn it into a delicious vegetarian dinner.
ingredients: 18 portobello mushroom caps (about 3 1/2 lbs.), stems and gills removed (see Tip)
1 tablespoon chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley, divided

name: Crispy Air-Fryer Pickle Chips
description: Use your air fryer to make these crispy, salty pickle chips for game day! The air fryer gives you a crispy crust, using much less oil than a traditional fryer. Enjoy these addictive little snacks on their own or with our Southern smoky, creamy dipping sauce.
ingredients: 1 (16 ounce) jar reduced-sodium dill pickle chips

name: Lemony Prosciutto-Wrapped Chicken Breasts
description: This ultra-quick recipe is as easy as it gets. Chicken breasts are wrapped in a slice of savory prosciutto, and cooked until crispy. While they rest, you whip up a simple lemony pan sauce that brings it all together. Serve with Brussels sprouts and toasted garlic bread to complete the meal.

name: Hard-Boiled Egg BLTs
description: Ditch the bread and use an egg to make these cute and tasty hard-boiled egg BLTs with bacon, avocado mayo and spinach. Serve these bites as an appetizer for any party--especially if you're hosting people who are following a low-carb diet.
ingredients: 4 slices thick-cut bacon, cooked and cut into thirds
¼ teaspoon ground pepper, plus more for garnish

name: Apple & Pecan Stuffed Butternut Squash
description: Let this cute little vegetarian stuffed squash side dish steal the show at the dinner table! Butternut squash is stuffed with a sweet and savory filling that gets a crispy finish under the broiler. If you can't find small butternut squash, honeynut squash is a great alternative. Shaped much like a butternut squash (but smaller), the honeynut squash has a deep orange skin and sweet flesh.
ingredients: 2 small (about 1 lb. each) butternut squash or honeynut squash
¾ cup finely chopped Honeycrisp apple

name: Turkey, Pasta & Vegetable Soup
description: This simple, clean turkey soup is full of vegetables and shredded turkey, making it perfect for after the holidays when you have leftovers on hand. Don't forget to save your Parmesan rinds! Adding one to a soup like this adds a rich umami flavor.
ingredients: 8 cups unsalted turkey broth or chicken broth

name: Chicken Cutlets with Creamy Spinach & Roasted Red Pepper Sauce
description: Serve these saucy, ultra-quick chicken cutlets over your favorite pasta. This easy dinner recipe is sure to become a new weeknight favorite.
ingredients: ½ cup finely chopped red onion
⅓ cup roasted red peppers, thinly sliced
⅓ cup sun-dried tomato halves, thinly sliced

name: Vegan "Pancit Bihon" with Spaghetti Squash
description: Umami-rich shiitake mushrooms take the place of meat, and spaghetti squash strands stand in for the traditional rice noodles, in this riff on the classic Filipino noodle dish pancit bihon. Serve alongside your favorite vegan main or add some cubed tofu to make it a meal.
ingredients: 1 small spaghetti squash (1 3/4-2 pounds), halved and seeds removed
2 cups shredded green vegetables such as cabbage, kale or bok choy

name: 20-Minute Chicken Cutlets & Zucchini Noodles with Creamy Tomato Sauce
description: This low-carb chicken dinner whips up in only 20 minutes, thanks to thin-cut chicken breast and packaged zucchini noodles. If you have a spiralizer and want to make your own zucchini noodles, use two medium zucchini.
ingredients: ½ cup finely chopped red onion

name: Banana, Raisin & Walnut Baked Oatmeal
description: How do you make oatmeal even easier? Bake it! Here we combine oats with milk and yogurt and flavor them with spices. Banana and raisins add natural sweetness. Once combined, the oatmeal is baked in the oven for a no-fuss breakfast the whole family will love.
ingredients: ¼ cup packed light brown sugar
1 large banana, halved lengthwise and sliced

name: Filipino Pancit Bihon
description: This healthy version of the classic Filipino noodle dish pancit bihon has plenty of vegetables, along with chicken and pork. And since it comes together in about a half-hour, this traditional party food can do double duty as an easy weeknight dinner.
ingredients: 8 ounces pork tenderloin, halved lengthwise and thinly sliced
8 ounces boneless, skinless chicken thighs, thinly sliced
1 ½ cups halved and sliced carrots
1 (8 ounce) package pancit bihon rice noodles (see Tip)

name: Cheesy Chaffle Sandwiches with Avocado & Bacon
description: You don't have to be on a low-carb or Keto diet to jump on the "chaffle" trend--chaffles are flourless waffles made from eggs and cheese. They may be gimmicky, but they're also delicious. For our chaffle sandwiches, we've added some crumbled bacon to the batter and used the chaffles to make sandwiches, filled with avocado and tomato. Enjoy a sandwich as a low-carb breakfast that also happens to be gluten-free.
ingredients: 1 ¼ cups shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
2 slices center-cut bacon, cooked and crumbled
4 large leaves butterhead lettuce, torn into 3-inch pieces

name: Shrimp-&-Grits-Stuffed Peppers
description: Shrimp and grits are stuffed inside bell peppers and baked in this healthy stuffed peppers recipe. Add the optional hot sauce if you want to give the filling some kick. Look for bell peppers--use any color--with even bottoms, so that they stand upright on their own when they're cooked.
ingredients: ¾ cup shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese
½ teaspoon hot sauce, such as Tabasco
1 pound peeled and deveined large shrimp (16-20 count), chopped

name: Chicken & Shiitake Dumplings in Tangy Chile-Oil Sauce
description: Serve these deliciously savory mushroom-and-chicken dumplings for Lunar New Year or any other special occasion. The sauce has just the right balance of sweet, spicy and tangy flavors.
ingredients: 8 ounces ground chicken, preferably thigh meat
4 dried shiitake mushrooms (1/2 ounce), rehydrated, stems removed and caps finely chopped (1/2 cup;" see Tips)
½ cup finely chopped scallions, green and white parts
⅛ teaspoon freshly ground white or black pepper
1 tablespoon Shaoxing rice wine or dry sherry
1 tablespoon canola oil or other neutral oil
¼ cup chopped fresh cilantro, for garnish

name: Everything-Bagel Zucchini Fries
description: The breading for these zucchini curly fries takes on the flavor of a classic everything bagel while the tangy, creamy sauce finishes off this fun appetizer that's perfect for a party or brunch. Store-bought everything seasoning can be loaded with salt, so it's best to make your own at home. Look for fresh and crisp-looking zucchini noodles in the produce section or make your own using a spiralizer. Soggy ones (or even the frozen variety) won't crisp up well.
ingredients: 4 ounces whipped cream cheese (about 2/3 cup), softened

name: Beef and Vegetable Sandwiches
description: Similar to sloppy joes but with more vegetables, these delicious sandwiches are made in the slow cooker.
ingredients: ⅓ cup no-salt-added tomato paste ( ½;" of a 6-ounce can)

name: Air-Fryer Cauliflower Gnocchi with Marinara Dipping Sauce
description: These tasty bites are perfect for anytime you need an easy appetizer or side dish. Cauliflower gnocchi get a crispy, cheesy coating on the outside and a tender middle in an air fryer. Marinara is the perfect complement for dipping.
ingredients: 2 (10 ounce) packages frozen cauliflower gnocchi, thawed, divided
½ cup grated Parmesan cheese, divided

name: Vegetarian Lettuce Wraps
description: Stuff crisp lettuce leaves with a savory filling inspired by PF Chang's famous lettuce wraps. These low-carb wraps made with tofu, mushrooms and daikon radish are an easy vegetarian dinner that beats takeout! Garnish the wraps with julienned carrots for added crunch.
ingredients: 8 large leaves Bibb or iceberg lettuce

name: Spinach-Feta Dip with Dill
description: Try this spinach dip, spiked with fresh dill and briny feta cheese, as a sandwich spread--or serve alongside crackers or crudité;".

name: Tofu Tacos
description: These quick vegan tacos, filled with a spicy tofu filling, make a perfect weeknight dinner. To keep them vegan, top them with shredded cabbage, fresh pico de gallo and guacamole. For vegetarians, add crumbled queso fresco.
ingredients: 1 (14 to 16 ounce) block extra-firm tofu, patted dry and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
¼ cup Shredded cabbage, pico de gallo and/or guacamole

name: Eat-the-Rainbow Chopped Salad with Basil & Mozzarella
description: This fresh and colorful salad has all of the delicious flavors you love from the classic caprese salad, plus even more healthy veggies. Double this and top each portion with 3 ounces grilled or roasted chicken to take it from a simple side to a quick main dish.
ingredients: ½ cup thinly sliced fresh basil

name: Greek Turkey Burgers with Portobello "Buns
description: This low-carb burger replaces the typical bun with roasted portobello mushroom caps. Creamy tzatziki and crisp sliced red onion add texture and flavor to this easy Greek-inspired burger recipe.
ingredients: 1 cup frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
8 large portobello mushrooms, stems removed (see Tip)

name: Crock-Pot Pineapple Chicken
description: This slow-cooker chicken with pineapple has a hint of ginger and sesame and is made with simple ingredients you may already have in your pantry! Look for fresh pineapple that's been peeled and cored already to make assembly even easier. Serve with brown rice to sop up the sweet and savory sauce.
ingredients: 1 ½ pounds boneless, skinless chicken thighs
1 large red bell pepper, cut into 1-inch pieces

name: Loaded Portobello Mushrooms
description: Think of these loaded portobello mushrooms as a low-carb version of a stuffed potato--Cheddar cheese, sour cream, scallions and bacon melted into portobello mushroom caps for a delicious and satisfying dinner. Serve them with a green salad to round out the meal.
ingredients: 4 portobello mushrooms (about 14 ounces), stems and gills removed (see Tip)
¾ cup shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese

name: Mexican Stuffed Portobello Mushrooms
description: We've taken some key burrito ingredients--black beans, corn and spicy pepper Jack cheese--and piled them into roasted portobello mushroom caps for a delicious and satisfying vegetarian dinner. Serve these with pico de gallo or any of your favorite burrito toppings!
ingredients: ⅛ teaspoon salt, plus 1/4 teaspoon, divided
4 portobello mushrooms (about 14 ounces), stems and gills removed (see Tip)
¼ cup finely chopped red onion
½ cup shredded pepper Jack cheese

name: Vegetarian Spring Egg Casserole
description: This easy egg casserole is filled with spring green vegetables and rustic whole-grain bread. It's perfect for a healthy vegetarian dinner or a springtime brunch. You can assemble it the night before and bake it in the morning when you're ready.
ingredients: 6 ounces fresh baby spinach (about 10 cups), chopped
¾ cup shredded whole-milk mozzarella cheese
8 ounces whole-grain crusty bread, cut into 1-inch cubes (about 5 cups)
1 (12 ounce) bunch asparagus, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces

name: Strawberry Poke Cake
description: What makes this poke cake so special? After this lightened-up cake is baked and cooled, it's poked with holes (hence the name!) that are filled with fresh pureed strawberries thickened with gelatin to prevent the cake from getting soggy. The end result? Sweet strawberry flavor inside and out!
ingredients: 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, plus 1/2 teaspoon, divided
Whole-milk vanilla Greek yogurt or whipped cream for garnish

name: Spinach & Mushroom Quiche
description: This healthy vegetarian quiche recipe is as simple as it gets. It's a quiche without the fussy crust! It's filled with sweet wild mushrooms and savory Gruyère cheese. Enjoy it for breakfast or brunch, or serve it with a light salad for lunch.
ingredients: 8 ounces sliced fresh mixed wild mushrooms such as cremini, shiitake, button and/or oyster mushrooms
1 ½ cups thinly sliced sweet onion
5 ounces fresh baby spinach (about 8 cups), coarsely chopped
1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves, plus more for garnish

name: Vegan Lemon Cake
description: You don't have to be vegan to enjoy this vegan lemon cake! It's moist, delicious and plenty lemony thanks to a combination of lemon juice and zest. A glaze made with lemon juice, confectioners' sugar and almond milk adds a sweet-tangy finish.
ingredients: ⅔ cup unsweetened almond milk, plus 1 tablespoon, divided

name: Slow-Cooker Cheesy Potatoes
description: What's not to love about this easy dump-and-go slow cooker recipe? It has potatoes and ooey-gooey cheese, all combined with a can of condensed cream of chicken soup! Whip up this kid-friendly side dish anytime you have a crowd to feed.
ingredients: 1 (32 ounce) package frozen cubed hash browns, thawed, or 7 cups cubed peeled potatoes
1 (15 ounce) can reduced-sodium condensed cream of chicken soup (such as Healthy Choice)
1 cup thinly sliced scallions, plus more for garnish
1 ½ cups shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese, divided
1 ½ cups shredded sharp Cheddar cheese, divided

name: Potato Kugel
description: Shredded potato, a few eggs and fresh herbs bake together for just under an hour. The result? A potato kugel with a crunchy top and bottom and a soft shredded-potato center. If you love your potato kugel crispy, don't skip the step of preheating the pan! Adding the potatoes to the hot pan will give you the crispy bottom that makes this recipe special.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley, divided

name: Chopped Salad with Shrimp, Apples & Pecans
description: Sweet and crunchy apples and celery combine with savory shrimp and nutty pecans in this easy, colorful dinner salad.
ingredients: 5 tablespoons reduced-fat sour cream or plain Greek yogurt

name: Mediterranean Broccoli Pasta Salad
description: We've loaded this healthy pasta salad with vegetables and flavors of the Mediterranean. Sun-dried tomatoes and a touch of lemon zest jazz up the dressing, while tender-crisp broccoli florets cook alongside the pasta, making assembly (and cleanup!) a breeze.
ingredients: ½ cup chopped red bell pepper
½ cup finely chopped sun-dried tomatoes in oil, drained

name: Instant Pot Brisket
description: Your Instant Pot is the perfect appliance to turn to if you want tender, juicy beef brisket done quickly. Here, we cut the brisket in half so it can fit inside the pot. After it's seared, it's cooked with plenty of onions that add body once blended into the flavorful, rich sauce. If you are making this brisket for Passover, look for products (like ketchup) that are labeled "Kosher for Passover.
ingredients: 1 (3 pound) beef brisket, cut in half crosswise
2 medium red onions, each cut into 8 wedges

name: Honey Walnut Shrimp
description: We gave a healthy spin to this popular takeout favorite. Walnuts are given a brown sugar coating that pairs nicely with sweet and savory shrimp. Serve the shrimp with rice and steamed veggies to make it a full, healthy meal. This ultra-quick dinner recipe is sure to become a new weeknight favorite.
ingredients: 1 pound jumbo peeled, deveined raw shrimp
2 cups hot cooked brown rice or white rice

name: Ham & Potato Casserole
description: This easy casserole is a kid-friendly recipe that the whole family will love. The simple flavor profile will complement just about anything and thanks to convenience products like preshredded potatoes, you won't spend hours in the kitchen mixing it up.
ingredients: 2 (20 ounce) packages refrigerated shredded hash browns or 10 cups shredded peeled potatoes
1 ¾ cups cubed ham steak or reduced-sodium deli ham (about 8 ounces)

name: Avocado Egg Salad
description: This quick recipe takes classic egg salad to the next level with the addition of creamy avocado. Serve it on a piece of toasted whole-grain bread or inside a tender lettuce leaf. Avocados brown quickly, so plan on making it no more than two hours before you plan to serve it.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh chives, plus more for garnish

name: Healthy Carrot Cake Muffins
description: Carrot cake meets muffins in this healthy breakfast recipe. Whole-wheat pastry flour along with plenty of carrots and toasted walnuts add fiber while applesauce keeps the muffins moist and helps cut back on sugar.
ingredients: ½ cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt, plus 2 tablespoons, divided
1 ½ cups grated carrots (about 3 medium carrots)
1 ½ ounces reduced-fat cream cheese, softened

name: Asparagus Casserole
description: Blanching the asparagus before assembling this casserole helps to maintain its vibrant green color. Feel free to skip that step if you're crunched for time, but the vegetables will be more army green.
ingredients: ½ cup panko breadcrumbs, preferably whole-wheat
3 pounds asparagus, trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces
½ cup shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese

name: Broccoli & Grape Salad
description: An unlikely pairing of grapes and broccoli creates an out-of-the-ordinary quick side dish. The sweet snappy bite of the grapes complements the crispy raw broccoli and onion, while the creamy dressing and toasted almonds pull it all together. Enjoy it for lunch or double the recipe for an easy potluck recipe everyone will love.
ingredients: 3 ½ cups very small broccoli florets
¼ cup whole-milk plain Greek yogurt

name: Zucchini Noodle Primavera
description: This primavera recipe cuts carbs by swapping out the pasta for zucchini "noodles." This quick vegetarian dinner is chock-full of colorful vegetables smothered in a light, creamy sauce. We like using prepackaged spiralized zucchini noodles to keep this recipe ultra-fast, but if you have a spiralizer and zucchini on hand, you can easily make your own.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons refrigerated basil pesto (such as Buitoni)
1 cup thinly sliced red bell pepper
2 (10 ounce) packages spiralized zucchini noodles (about 6 cups;" see Tip)

name: Sautéed Cabbage
description: This easy side dish takes only 20 minutes from start to finish. You can enjoy sautéed cabbage simply cooked with butter and onion or jazz it up with one of the variations (see below). Enjoy all the versions of this quick side dish with roasted pork or chicken.
ingredients: 1 small head cabbage, quartered lengthwise, cored and cut into 1/2-inch-thick wedges

name: One-Pan Chicken & Asparagus Bake
description: In this chicken and asparagus recipe, we use one baking sheet to whip up dinner quickly, veggies included. Pounding the chicken thin helps it cook quickly alongside the carrots and potatoes, with asparagus rounding out the meal. This is one quick dinner recipe you'll be returning to again and again.
ingredients: 2 (8 ounce) boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut in half crosswise
12 ounces baby Yukon Gold potatoes, halved lengthwise
8 ounces carrots, diagonally sliced into 1-inch pieces

name: Irish Beef Stew
description: If you're looking for a healthy beef stew recipe that delivers on comfort, look no further. This one-pot Irish beef stew is packed with veggies and rich meaty flavor. Make this Irish beef stew recipe with Guinness--a dark, malty Irish stout--to keep it authentic. Serve with a side of Irish soda bread to sop up the leftovers.
ingredients: 2 ¼ pounds boneless chuck roast, trimmed and cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
3 medium carrots, diagonally sliced into 1-inch pieces
1 (12 fluid ounce) bottle stout beer (such as Guinness)
1 ½ pounds baby Yukon Gold potatoes, halved
2 tablespoons chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley, plus more for garnish

name: Shepherd's Pie with Cauliflower Topping
description: In this easy shepherd's pie recipe, we keep the carbs in check by using creamy mashed cauliflower in place of mashed potatoes. The ground beef filling is cooked in the same skillet used for baking the pie, making assembly (and cleanup) a breeze.
ingredients: 1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added diced tomatoes, drained
3 tablespoons chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley, divided
1 tablespoon all-purpose flour or gluten-free all-purpose flour
8 cups cauliflower florets (from 1 large head cauliflower)

name: Garlic-Parmesan Asparagus
description: In this garlic-Parmesan-crusted asparagus recipe, we combine Parmesan cheese, whole-wheat panko breadcrumbs and walnuts for a crispy topping that's baked over asparagus. Roasting the asparagus spears in a hot oven keeps them tender-crisp in this quick side dish!
ingredients: ½ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese

name: Pickled Asparagus
description: In this unique asparagus recipe, we swap out the cucumbers typically used for pickling and opt for asparagus spears instead, which remain tender-crisp even after soaking up the sweet-tangy pickling liquid. Serve these pickled asparagus spears as part of an appetizer spread, chop them up to use as a salad topper or munch on them as a zingy, refreshing snack.

name: Creamy Mushroom, Chicken & Asparagus Bake
description: This comforting weeknight casserole recipe features plenty of mushrooms and asparagus combined with chicken and brown rice and a creamy Parmesan cheese sauce. Whip this up anytime you have leftover chicken or cooked brown rice to spare.
ingredients: 1 ½ tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, plus 1 1/2 teaspoons, divided
2 (8 ounce) packages sliced fresh button mushrooms
½ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese, divided
1 pound fresh asparagus, trimmed and cut into 1 inch pieces
1 (8.8 ounce) pouch precooked microwaveable whole-grain brown rice or 1 1/2 cups cooked brown rice

name: Cheesy Asparagus
description: In this cheesy baked asparagus recipe, asparagus spears are roasted whole smothered in a creamy, cheesy garlic sauce. This low-carb side dish is a great way to entice picky eaters to eat their veggies! Pair it with roast chicken or steak.
ingredients: ¼ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese

name: One-Bowl Irish Stout Cake
description: This Irish stout cake can't get any easier: everything is mixed in one bowl, poured into the cake pan and is in the oven in 15 minutes flat. Lining the cake pan with parchment paper makes removing the cake easy and helps with cleanup. After this rich chocolate cake has cooled, enjoy it with the espresso- and Irish whiskey-laced whipped cream topping.
ingredients: 1 ¼ cups Irish-style stout beer (such as Guinness)

name: Mediterranean Cabbage Soup
description: This vegan soup recipe is surprisingly easy to make. The flavorful broth is spiked with garlic, tomatoes and a hint of coriander. Just slice and dice your cabbage and fennel and you'll be enjoying this quick healthy soup in no time.
ingredients: 1 cup sliced fennel, fronds reserved for garnish
1 (15 ounce) can no-salt-added diced tomatoes with basil, garlic and oregano
1 small head green cabbage (1 1/2 lbs.), chopped
1 (15 ounce) can unsalted cannellini beans, rinsed

name: Loaded Cauliflower Bowls
description: Enjoy these comforting roasted cauliflower bowls topped with cheese, bacon, scallions and sour cream on their own, or add grilled chicken or shrimp to make it a full meal.
ingredients: ¾ cup shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese, divided

name: Spinach & Artichoke-Stuffed Portobello Mushrooms
description: Stuffed mushrooms and spinach-artichoke dip come together in this quick vegetarian recipe. Serve these cheesy stuffed mushrooms with a big salad for a satisfying and healthy dinner.
ingredients: 4 large portobello mushrooms (about 14 ounces), stems and gills removed (see Tip)
1 (5 ounce) package baby spinach, roughly chopped
1 (14 ounce) can artichoke hearts, rinsed, squeezed dry and chopped
¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus more for garnish

name: Flat-Belly Soup
description: This vegan soup recipe is chock-full of colorful vegetables. All those veggies give this soup plenty of fiber, upping the satisfaction factor while keeping calories low, which can aid in weight loss and lead to--yes--a flatter belly. Vibrant turmeric adds a golden color to the flavorful broth, while cumin and ginger give it a bright, fresh finish. And the best part? This healthy soup recipe takes only 20 minutes, start to finish!
ingredients: 1 ½ teaspoons finely chopped fresh ginger
3 cups stemmed and chopped rainbow chard

name: Low-Carb Broccoli & Cheddar Casserole
description: This simple cheesy casserole is comforting while keeping the carbs in check. Serve it alongside roasted chicken or pork.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups shredded extra-sharp Cheddar cheese, divided

name: Brûléed Blood Oranges with Yogurt & Cardamom
description: Fresh blood oranges are so sweet they almost create their own caramel under the broiler--a little brown sugar on top seals the deal. A hint of cardamom enhances their aroma. Once you scoop out and eat the caramelized sections, squeeze the remaining blood orange juice over the yogurt.

name: Afghan Spiced Tea (Kahwah)
description: Afghans sip this spiced tea throughout the day.
ingredients: 2 teaspoons loose black or green tea
1 teaspoon Sugar or honey to taste

name: Blood Orange Upside-Down Cake
description: Upside-down cakes have a built-in wow factor with a layer of glacéed-looking fruit. This cake includes almond flour to give it richness and added moisture, plus zest for a more intense layer of blood orange flavor.
ingredients: ¼ cup packed light brown sugar
¾ cup almond flour or fine cornmeal
½ cup low-fat plain Greek yogurt

name: Fish Tostadas with Blood Orange Salsa
description: The salsa that goes with these tostadas is powerfully sweet and tangy but won't drown out the delicate-flavored white fish. Try pairing the sauce with chicken or pork as well.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons grapeseed or canola oil, divided
1 pound skinless Pacific halibut or cod, cut into 4 portions
1 tablespoon chile-lime seasoning or rub, such as Tajín

name: Broiled Lamb Chops with Charred Blood Oranges
description: In the south of France, lamb is often grilled on a bed of fresh rosemary and anointed with citrus juice. This lamb chop recipe takes inspiration from that tradition with a wet rub made with lots of garlic plus rosemary and blood orange. Besides being a beautiful garnish, the charred blood oranges are served to squeeze over the lamb for a final flourish of citrus flavor.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh rosemary, plus 6 sprigs
