description: A fried egg in a half-baguette with harissa, olive, and tomato slice — the fast Tunisian breakfast eaten before school or work.

name: Fruit Salad with Raïb (Fresh Yogurt)
description: Seasonal diced fruits — orange, apple, banana — served alongside a pot of cold Raïb, the Tunisian fermented milk yogurt.

name: Chorba Lsan Asfour (Vermicelli Soup)
description: A comforting light Tunisian soup made with tiny pasta (bird tongue), tomato, celery, and either chicken or beef.

name: Harissa Pasta (Macarouna Harissa)
description: Spaghetti cooked in a simple Tunisian harissa-tomato sauce — the weeknight dinner in most Tunisian households.

name: Kafteji (Traditional Fried Version) — Smaller Portion
description: The classic Tunisian fried vegetable and egg dish — a smaller, controlled portion for dinner.

name: Sardines Grillées with Harissa and Salad
description: Whole fresh sardines seasoned with chermoula and grilled on a pan or barbecue, served with a Tunisian salad.

name: Rechta (Tunisian Flat Pasta with Chicken Broth)
description: Homemade flat pasta strips cooked in a light chicken and onion broth, scented with cinnamon and rosewater.

name: Steamed Chicken with Couscous and Harissa Broth
description: A lighter single-serving version of the Friday couscous — steamed grain with one piece of chicken and a small cup of broth.

name: Tajine Tunisien (Egg and Cheese Bake)
description: Not the Moroccan version — the Tunisian Tajine is a baked egg, cheese, and meat frittata, served in slices.

name: Dates with Almonds
description: Three Deglet Nour dates each stuffed with an almond — the most accessible and culturally natural Tunisian snack.

name: Plain Yogurt with a Drizzle of Honey
description: A small pot of plain Tunisian yogurt with a teaspoon of honey — low-calorie, high-protein afternoon snack.

name: Makroudh (1 piece)
description: A single traditional Tunisian semolina pastry filled with date paste and fried or baked — one piece as a controlled sweet snack.

name: Watermelon Slice (Summer)
description: A generous slice of cold watermelon — the quintessential Tunisian summer snack, zero prep, extremely hydrating.

name: Lait Ribot with a Date
description: A glass of cold Lait Ribot (Tunisian fermented buttermilk) with one date — a probiotic-rich snack found in every Tunisian fridge.

name: Salade Tunisienne with Grilled Chicken
description: A classic finely chopped Tunisian cucumber and tomato salad served with grilled chicken breast.

name: Ojja with Tuna
description: A quick and spicy Tunisian tomato and egg stew topped with canned tuna.

name: Loubia with Beef
description: A hearty high-fiber tomato-based stew with white beans and lean beef.

name: Chorba Frik with Chicken
description: A traditional Tunisian crushed green wheat soup made with chicken and celery.

name: Couscous Bel Khodra with Chicken
description: A balanced portion of Tunisian couscous with carrots, zucchini, pumpkin, and lean chicken.

name: Market 3das (Tunisian Lentil Stew)
description: A thick, nutritious lentil stew with carrots, garlic, and cumin.

name: Salade Mechouia with Grilled Turkey
description: Grilled and mashed peppers and tomatoes served with lean turkey.

name: Plat Tunisien (Healthy Version)
description: A deconstructed sandwich plate: tuna, boiled eggs, small potato, harissa.

name: Tunisian Macarouna with Chicken
description: Spaghetti in a rich garlicky Tunisian tomato sauce with chicken.

name: Healthy Oven-Baked Kafteji
description: A lighter baked version of traditional Kafteji with eggs.

name: Kamounia (Beef and Liver Stew)
description: A rich cumin-scented stew with beef chunks and liver.

name: Jelbana with Chicken (Pea Stew)
description: A savory stew of green peas, artichoke hearts, and chicken.

name: Brik au Four with Salad
description: Baked brik filled with tuna, egg, and parsley, served with green salad.

name: Rouz Jerbi (Djerbian Rice)
description: Traditional steamed rice with spinach, parsley, and lean chicken.

name: Omelette Tastira
description: Eggs beaten into sautéed tomatoes and peppers.

name: Chicken and Potato Traybake (Koucha Djaj)
description: Chicken and potato wedges in Tunisian spices, roasted in the oven.
